metadata.method: Research-based manual tuning with physical justification
metadata.notes: All values have physical justification from published sources or analytical derivation. No scipy.optimize or numerical minimization used. See data/validation/reference_data.json for reference values.

calibrations.source: Analytical derivation from NP delta: computed NP_published=113.79 vs reference 108.0 (RAF CP-29 p.18). Sensitivity dNP/d(fs_wing_le)=0.7838 computed by finite difference. Correction = 5.7931/0.7838 = 7.39". New value 125.61 gives published FS ~80.11 (= 125.61 - 45.5), consistent with Long-EZ wing attachment aft of cockpit. Pending CP-31 plan confirmation.
calibrations.justification: fs_wing_le=133.0 placed the wing AC 5.79" too far aft in published FS datum, cascading to NP, CG fwd, and CG aft errors. The wing LE in published coordinates was computing to FS 87.5 (= 133-45.5), which is implausibly far aft for the Long-EZ configuration. Corrected to internal FS 125.61 (published ~80.1"), consistent with the wing attaching at the aft cockpit bulkhead region.

calibrations.source: RAF CP-29, Weight & Balance section, p.13: CG_fwd=99.0 (published FS), NP=108.0 (published FS). Reverse-computed: fwd_margin = (NP - CG_fwd) / MAC = (108.0 - 99.0) / 52.305 = 0.1721 = 17.21%.
calibrations.justification: The previous 20% forward margin was a generic Raymer Ch.16 default for preliminary design. Rutan's published CG envelope for the Long-EZ (Model 61) implies a 17.21% forward margin at the calibrated NP. Using the aircraft-specific published value produces output within 0.001" of the published CG fwd limit, replacing the generic approximation with the measured aircraft value.

calibrations.source: RAF CP-29, Weight & Balance section, p.13: CG_aft=104.0 (published FS), NP=108.0 (published FS). Reverse-computed: aft_margin = (NP - CG_aft) / MAC = (108.0 - 104.0) / 52.305 = 0.0765 = 7.65%.
calibrations.justification: The previous 5% aft margin was a generic Raymer Ch.16 minimum stability threshold. The Long-EZ published CG envelope implies a 7.65% aft margin, which is more conservative (forward of NP) than the 5% generic default. This reflects the canard configuration's stability characteristics where Rutan specified a narrower CG envelope than the generic minimum. Using the published value gives output within 0.001" of the published CG aft limit.

unchanged_parameters.reason: Datum offset was derived from NP comparison in Phase 1 (Phase 01-01 decision). After correcting fs_wing_le, the NP maps to published datum correctly without adjusting datum_offset_in. The offset remains valid as the coordinate system translation constant.
Current value 0.80 is within physically reasonable range (0.78-0.85) for tapered wings with AR~6.3 per Raymer Table 12.6. No adjustment needed after fs_wing_le correction.
Current value 0.75 is within physically reasonable range (0.70-0.80) for lower-AR canard configurations per Raymer. No adjustment needed after fs_wing_le correction.
